Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Sanskrit and shapes in ether

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2008-01-14 · 5 answers
I heard the other day in a lecture by Bhakti Vijnana Goswami Maharaj that written sanskrit is actually what you see when you say the word. For example if you say Hare, the corresponding sanskrit shape appears in ether although we cannot see it.

He also mentioned that there was a scientist that captured this shapes and forms with a machine and was amazed of it.

Anyone knows where to find more about this?
user [38] · 2008-01-14
Look for "tonoscope" (name of the machine).
user [154] · 2008-01-14
[quote][cite] abhiram:[/cite]I heard the other day in a lecture by Bhakti Vijnana Goswami Maharaj that written sanskrit is actually what you see when you say the word. For example if you say Hare, the corresponding sanskrit shape appears in ether although we cannot see it.

He also mentioned that there was a scientist that captured this shapes and forms with a machine and was amazed of it.

Anyone knows where to find more about this?[/quote]

You are of course aware that there are different scripts for Sanskrit and Prakrit.
user [19] · 2008-01-18
nice but the tonoscope is supposed to display a yantra and not the typical sanskrit i was hoping for.
:(
user [251] · 2008-02-24
The system is called cymatics - where the sounds are visible in the forms of shapes more like yantras. That is why one should only utter beneficial words and sounds otherwise they will be polluting the eather.

In the scriptures it is mentioned that where one is chanting the holy names of the Lord, that place becomes purified. That is why Harinama Sankirtan is very important in this age of kali to counteract the negative effects. The seers of the past and acharyas and incarnations of the Lord come to propagate the Holy names to save the conditioned living entitites from the onslaught of the Kali-yuga. Harinama Sankirtan Ki Jaya. Please chant the holy names. Hare Krsna Hare Krsna Krsan Krsna Hare Hare, Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Thank You very much.
user [247] · 2008-02-27
what shape would we see thence and forth.. many mandalas beautifull displaying one day by all sankirtaners Kii - JAYA !!
